title: symmetric put/get-without-field design — defend against reverting
description: Changing put-without-field from an error to accepting a JSON object is the correct design. The old API had a structural asymmetry: get-without-field returns JSON, put-without-field was always an error. The new API is symmetric: get returns JSON, put accepts JSON. A contributor might argue "require ?field= always, put without field is ambiguous." Reason to keep: the full-JSON path is the natural way to write a multi-field KV secret atomically and is the complement of get-without-field. The old hard error forced users to add ?field= even when they wanted to manage the full map, which is the correct use case for put-without-field.
